incoming_email: Hi, we are cancelling our account. Will this delete all our historical email threads? We need to keep a record of past chats.
sent_reply:
Hi John,

Rest assured, your emails are safe! Because Hiver runs on top of Gmail, all your emails remain in your Gmail accounts even after you cancel. Only the Hiver metadata (assignees, notes, tags) will be archived on our end, but the actual email threads will never be deleted from your Gmail.

Best regards,
Alice
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hello support, if we cancel our Hiver subscription, do we lose access to all the emails in our shared folders? Or do they stay in Gmail?
sent_reply:
Hello Sarah,

Cancelling Hiver does not delete any of your emails from Gmail. The email threads will stay exactly where they are in your inbox. You will only lose access to the Hiver sidebar and features like email assignment and shared drafts. Let me know if you have other questions!

Best,
Bob
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hey Hiver, we're planning to cancel. Does cancellation delete the email data from Gmail, or just the Hiver labels?
sent_reply:
Hi Michael,

Good question! All your historical email data remains in Gmail. Hiver only overlays collaborative features on top of Gmail, so your core emails are never touched. You can cancel safely without losing your customer history.

Regards,
Charlie
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Dear support, please clarify what happens to our historical emails when we terminate our subscription. Will the emails be deleted?
sent_reply:
Hi Emily,

Thank you for asking. When you cancel, all emails remain in your Gmail folders. We do not delete any email content. The Hiver sharing layers are removed, but the original emails stay in your account. Let us know if you need help with the cancellation process.

Best,
David
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hi, I'm new to Hiver. How do I assign an email to one of my teammates? I can't find the assign button.
sent_reply:
Hi John,

Welcome to Hiver! To assign an email, open the thread in Gmail. You will see the Hiver sidebar on the right. Click the 'Assignee' dropdown field, select or search for your colleague, and click save. The email will automatically appear in their 'Assigned to me' tab.

Best,
Alice
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hello support, how do I delegate a conversation to someone else? I need a quick step-by-step guide.
sent_reply:
Hello Sarah,

Glad to have you onboard! You can assign an email by clicking the 'Assign' dropdown in the Hiver panel on the right side of the open email. Just choose your colleague's name, and they'll be notified immediately. Let me know if you would like a quick screenshare demo!

Best,
Bob
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hey team, where is the option to assign an email to a teammate? I see the shared inbox but not the assign options.
sent_reply:
Hi Michael,

To assign an email: 1. Open the thread in Gmail. 2. Look at the Hiver panel on the right. 3. Click the dropdown under 'Assignee' and pick a team member. You can also assign it to yourself by clicking the 'Claim' button at the top.

Regards,
Charlie
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Dear support, could you explain how the email assignment feature works? How do I assign a ticket to a colleague?
sent_reply:

To delegate a conversation, open the thread and use the Hiver widget on the right. Select your colleague from the 'Assignee' field. They will receive a notification and the email will show up in their Hiver inbox. Let us know if you need help with setting up auto-assignment filters.

Best,

Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hi, how do I configure Hiver's Zapier integration so it only triggers when an email is assigned to a specific tag, like 'Refund'?
sent_reply:
Hi John,

Yes, absolutely! In your Zapier setup, choose 'New Email Tagged' as the Hiver trigger. Then, in the trigger configuration, select 'Refund' from the tag list. This ensures the Zap only runs when that specific tag is applied. Let me know if you need help with subsequent Salesforce steps!

Best,
Alice
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hello, does the Hiver Zapier trigger support custom fields or tags? We want to filter events before sending them to Salesforce.
sent_reply:
Hello Sarah,

Yes, the Hiver Zapier integration exposes tags and assignees. You can add a 'Filter by Zapier' step right after the Hiver trigger. In the filter, set the condition to match the Hiver 'tag' or 'assignee' field before sending data to Salesforce. Let me know if you need a walkthrough.

Best,
Bob
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Hey team, we're building a Zap using Hiver. Can we filter based on Hiver labels? We need to route specific tagged emails to Jira.
sent_reply:
Hi Michael,

Yes, Hiver passes all labels and tags to Zapier! When setting up your Zap, use the 'New Email Tagged' trigger and choose your Jira-routing tag. This will filter out other emails automatically. Let us know if you need help!

Regards,
Charlie
Hiver Support

incoming_email: Dear support, how do we set up conditional filters in Zapier for Hiver emails? We only want to trigger Zaps for resolved emails.
sent_reply:
Hi Emily,

To trigger Zaps only for resolved emails, use the Hiver 'Email Status Changed' trigger in Zapier. Then, add a Zapier filter step where 'New Status' exactly matches 'Resolved'. This will filter out open or pending status updates. Let us know if you need anything else.

Best,
David
Hiver Support
